note: Sprint 5.8: adds Useful Compute cross-worker replay validator v0.1 and bumps result schema to v0.2. Result schema now separates compute_output_sha256 (hash of the pure technical task output, independent of worker_id, used for cross-worker matching) from worker_result_id (16-hex per-submission id binding request_id+worker_id+compute_sha+elapsed). scripts/trinity/useful_compute_worker.py refactored: _compute_seed no longer mixes worker_id into the placeholder task output. scripts/trinity/useful_compute_replay_validator.py groups loaded results by compute_output_sha256, dedupes worker_id, emits trinity-useful-compute-validation/v0.1 report with status in {accepted, mismatch, insufficient_workers, rejected, manual_review} and records lessons (overclaim_risk/insufficient_evidence/duplicate_candidate) into trinity_error_memory when applicable. website/trinity-useful-compute.html gains Step 5 Validate Replay Results section (local groupby + CLI command surface; no network, no sensitive inputs). docs/TRINITY_USEFUL_COMPUTE_REPLAY_VALIDATOR_V01.md added. Result file naming includes worker_result_id so multiple workers can drop into the same dir without clobbering. No automatic payout, no wallet access, no network, no consensus, no RPC, no on-chain registration. Tests: 37 new across tests/trinity/test_{useful_compute_replay_validator,useful_compute_validation_schema,useful_compute_replay_web_safety}.py; trinity suite 275/275 PASS with materials-engine-private present.
